name: Form Workflow
description: Automated workflow: Form Workflow. This workflow integrates 6 different services: stickyNote, formTrigger, stopAndError, slack, googleSheets. It contains 15 nodes and follows best practices for error handling and security.

parameters.completionMessage:
Many thanks for taking the time to complete this short survey. A community representative will contact you shortly!

We hope you enjoy the newsletter and please feel free to contact us at <EMAIL> should you have any questions.

Go back to <HOMEPAGE>.

nodes.notes: This form node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

nodes.name: Sticky Note1

parameters.content:
## 1. Easy Lead Capture with n8n Forms
[Learn more about Form Triggers]({{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }}

Previously, the n8n form experience was quite limited as you were only given one form page to work with. Now with multi-page forms where its possible to link between them, you can get creative on providing a richer form experience for your users.

Here, we start by capturing the most important information first - the user's email address - and saving it to our Google Sheet. We can then follow-up with an optional short onboarding survey to capture more details about the user if they are willing.

nodes.notes: This stickyNote node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

nodes.name: Sticky Note

parameters.content:
## 2. Follow-on Short Survey via Multi-Step Forms
[Read more about n8n Form node]({{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }}

Multi-page forms are built by simply chaining a series of n8n form nodes. n8n handles the progress of the form for you - ie. proceeds when the form validates and the user submits the form - which makes it easier to build as you don't need to add additional nodes in between.

After the user provides their email, we present an optional short survey to capture additional details. This step is made of 3 form nodes capturing profession, experience and goals of the user which is then saved to the same row in the google sheet.

nodes.notes: This stickyNote node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

nodes.name: Sticky Note2
nodes.notes: This stickyNote node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

nodes.name: Sticky Note4

parameters.content:
## Try It Out!

### This template builds a simple newsletter signup form with a follow-on short survey entirely in n8n! Taking full advantage of n8n's new multi-page form functionality, it's easy to build impactful forms to serve your business.

### How it works
* Our flow begins with a form trigger to capture a newsletter signup and the user's email is captured into a google sheet. Google Sheet is used for demonstration purposes but this could be any database.
* Multi-page forms allow you to continue the onboarding experience with a short survey. 3 form nodes are chained to capture more details from the user which update the same row in the google sheet.
* Finally, a form ending node shows a customised completion screen for our user.

Check out the example sheet here: {{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }}


### Need Help?
Join the [Discord]({{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }} or ask in the [Forum]({{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }}

Happy Hacking!

nodes.notes: This stickyNote node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

parameters.content:
## 2. Follow-on Short Survey via Multi-Step Forms
[Read more about n8n Form node]({{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }}

Multi-page forms are built by simply chaining a series of n8n form nodes. n8n handles the progress of the form for you - ie. proceeds when the form validates and the user submits the form - which makes it easier to build as you don't need to add additional nodes in between.

After the user provides their email, we present an optional short survey to capture additional details. This step is made of 3 form nodes capturing profession, experience and goals of the user which is then saved to the same row in the google sheet.

## 1. Easy Lead Capture with n8n Forms
[Learn more about Form Triggers]({{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }}

Previously, the n8n form experience was quite limited as you were only given one form page to work with. Now with multi-page forms where its possible to link between them, you can get creative on providing a richer form experience for your users.

Here, we start by capturing the most important information first - the user's email address - and saving it to our Google Sheet. We can then follow-up with an optional short onboarding survey to capture more details about the user if they are willing.

## Try It Out!

### This template builds a simple newsletter signup form with a follow-on short survey entirely in n8n! Taking full advantage of n8n's new multi-page form functionality, it's easy to build impactful forms to serve your business.

### How it works
* Our flow begins with a form trigger to capture a newsletter signup and the user's email is captured into a google sheet. Google Sheet is used for demonstration purposes but this could be any database.
* Multi-page forms allow you to continue the onboarding experience with a short survey. 3 form nodes are chained to capture more details from the user which update the same row in the google sheet.
* Finally, a form ending node shows a customised completion screen for our user.

Check out the example sheet here: {{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }}


### Need Help?
Join the [Discord]({{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }} or ask in the [Forum]({{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }}

Happy Hacking!

options.formDescription:
Thanks <name>!

prerequisites: An active n8n instance installed locally, in a Docker container, or hosted on n8n Cloud.
Basic familiarity with importing JSON templates in n8n.
setupInstructions: Download the JSON configuration for this workflow.
Ensure you set up credentials for all connected integrations.
Test your trigger and run execution.
requiredCredentials: google Sheets  O Auth2  A P I details
configurationSteps: Verify the settings of the trigger node "Sign Up Form".
Open the "About You" node (n8n-nodes-base.form) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Your Interests" node (n8n-nodes-base.form) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Join Beta Testers" node (n8n-nodes-base.form) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Capture More Info" Google Sheets node.
Ensure you select the spreadsheet file and specific tab/sheet from the dropdown menus.
Map the input JSON keys to your target sheet columns.
Open the "Capture Email" Google Sheets node.
Ensure you select the spreadsheet file and specific tab/sheet from the dropdown menus.
Map the input JSON keys to your target sheet columns.
Open the "Show Completion Screen" node (n8n-nodes-base.form) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note1" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note2" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note4" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Notify New Signup!" Slack node.
Select the Channel or User ID where message notifications should be delivered.
Open the "Error Handler" node (n8n-nodes-base.stopAndError) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
No global variables node (Set node) is present. If you need dynamic constants, add a Set node after the trigger.
testingSteps: Click the "Test step" or "Listen for test event" button on your Trigger node.
Send a test trigger from your source service (e.g. fill a test form or trigger a webhook trigger).
Verify that the node canvas lights up green, indicating successful data flow.
Open the final destination node to confirm that output records were saved correctly.
instructions.expectedResult: Your Slack channel will receive customized real-time notifications when the automation executes.
troubleshooting: Check credential warning symbols. Most workflow issues stem from expired OAuth connections or missing API permissions.
Verify that the data keys coming from your Trigger node match the exact names mapped in subsequent nodes (e.g., {{ $json.body.email }}).
Open the Execution history tab in n8n to view logs and determine which node failed.
securityNotes: Never hardcode API keys, passwords, or authentication bearer tokens in the plain text fields of node parameters.
If you download or share this workflow JSON, sanitize any sensitive parameters or configuration nodes.
customizationIdeas: Add an email node at the end to notify you of successful executions.
Incorporate an AI LLM node to summarize trigger payloads before writing them to integrations.
